project.objective: When and where did William Hubert Ferber die?

questions.question: When and where did William Hubert Ferber die?
questions.rationale: The project objective is to determine the date and place of William Hubert Ferber's death. This single-event question targeting his death is the direct decomposition of the objective. Death records, obituaries, cemetery records, and related vital records are the primary record types to search. No prerequisite questions need resolution first.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: All primary Ohio death record repositories for the relevant period were searched: Ohio Deaths 1908-1953 (negative — death predates mandatory registration), Ohio Death Index 1908-2007 (negative), Ohio County Death Records 1840-2001 (positive — 'Wm. Ferber' d. 11 March 1903 with matching parents), and 1910 census (corroborating — Emma widowed). Confident identification achieved. Remaining plan items skipped as not needed.
exhaustive_declaration.stop_criteria: Death record found with parent names uniquely matching tree persons GCD9-9X1 and G7J1-QF2. 1910 census independently corroborates via Emma's widowed status. No conflicts in evidence.

items.rationale: Ohio began mandatory statewide death registration in 1908. FamilySearch collection 'Ohio, Deaths, 1908-1953' (id: 1307272) is fully indexed and contains death certificates with date, county, and place of death. William H. Ferber, born December 1869 in Ohio, was last documented alive in Cincinnati city directories ~1910. His wife Emma died in 1948 and son Charles in 1967, suggesting William likely died between 1911 and ~1948. This is the highest-probability single source for the death date and place.

FamilySearch 'Ohio, Death Index, 1908-1932, 1938-1944, and 1958-2007' (id: 1949341) is a complementary index covering a wider span than the death-certificate collection. Useful if the full-certificate search yields no hit due to spelling variation or if William died outside the 1908-1953 window. Searches variant spellings (Faerber, Farber, etc.).

The 1910 US Federal Census (taken April 1910) will confirm whether William H. Ferber was alive and residing in Cincinnati at that date, and will supply his occupation and household composition. If he does not appear in 1910, it suggests death between 1900 and 1910. If he does appear, the search window opens to post-1910.

The 1920 US Federal Census will determine if William survived past April 1920 and, if so, his residence, occupation, and household. Combined with the 1910 result this narrows the death window by a decade. If Emma appears as a widow in 1920, that fixes his death before January 1920.

Ancestry holds 'Ohio, Death Certificates, 1908-1953' and the broader Ohio death index with different coverage windows than FamilySearch. If FamilySearch yields no match, Ancestry may have the certificate or index entry under a variant spelling. Ancestry also provides access to earlier Hamilton County records. Search as a fallback on the primary death certificate search.

Cincinnati city directories (Ancestry 'U.S. City Directories, 1822-1995') list residents annually. William appears in directories through 1910; systematic coverage of 1911-1935 will pinpoint the year he stops appearing, bracketing the death window to a narrow range before the death certificate search is exhausted. This is especially useful if he died during the gap years (1933-1937) not covered by the FamilySearch death index.

The Social Security Death Index (FamilySearch 'United States Social Security Death Index') covers deaths of individuals who had a Social Security number — enrollment began 1936. If William was still alive in 1936, he may have obtained an SSN and would appear in the SSDI. This narrows the death date and provides the last known state of residence.

FAN item: William's wife Emma Becker Ferber died 7 December 1948 in Dayton, Campbell County, Kentucky. Her Kentucky death certificate (Ancestry 'Kentucky, U.S., Death Records, 1852-1965') will state her marital status (widowed vs. married) and — if widowed — may record her husband's name and date/place of his death. This is an indirect but potentially precise source for William's death that cross-checks whatever the Ohio records show.

log.notes: 47 results returned; none confidently match William Hubert Ferber b. Dec 1869, Cincinnati. Top-ranked hits are mentions of 'William Farber/Furber' on relatives' records or a different person. A 'Farber' with no given name died Cincinnati 9 Mar 1911 (rank 7). Will try Faerber variant and read top candidates before concluding negative.

Same 47 results as Ferber search. Top new hit is 'William E. Faerber' as principal (death record). Middle initial E not H but worth reading to confirm or rule out. No confirmed match for William H. Ferber yet.

No match for William H. Ferber b. Dec 1869 Cincinnati. 'William Henry Ferber' record (V23C-WK3) is a different person b. 1920 d. 2006. Index covers 1908-1932, 1938-1944, 1958-2007 — gap years 1933-1937 and 1945-1957 may explain absence. Proceeding to census records to bracket death window.

Emma Ferber listed as WIDOWED in 1910 census, Cincinnati Ward 21. Household: Gerhard Ferber (father-in-law, b.1821 Germany), Gustave Ferber (b.1857), Chas H Ferber (son, b.1892). No William in household. Confirms William H. Ferber died before April 15, 1910. William H. Ferber himself absent from all 1910 census searches — consistent with pre-April 1910 death.

Narrowed search: Ohio Deaths 1908–1953 (1307272), Ferber/William, death 1908–1912, Cincinnati. 32 results; none match William H. Ferber b. Dec 1869. Top results are other Ferbers or wrong-given-name variants. Combined with log_001 and log_002, collection 1307272 yields no indexed death certificate for William H. Ferber. He may have died before Ohio mandatory registration (pre-Jan 1908) or his record is filed under an unrecognized name form.

Surname-only "Ferber" search, Ohio Deaths 1908-1953, death 1906-1912, no given name. 1043 results but all 50 examined are Ferbers mentioned as relatives on other people's death certificates — not as the principal deceased. No record title "Entry for William Ferber" or variant. Pattern confirms name-variant issue or pre-1908 death. Next: isPrincipal=true search to isolate certificates where a Ferber is the actual deceased, and try Ohio County Death Records collection 2128172.

isPrincipal=true search: principal Ferber/Farber/Fieber deaths in Hamilton County Ohio 1906-1912. All 14 results reviewed; none match William H. Ferber b. Dec 1869 Ohio. The only male Cincinnati Hamilton deaths are "Farber" (d. 9 Mar 1911 — AFTER the April 1910 census showing Emma already widowed, so excluded) and "Gustavus Adolphus Fieber" (b. Sep 1847 Germany, wrong person). Confirms no indexed death certificate for William H. Ferber in Ohio Deaths 1908-1953 under any Ferber/Farber/Furber/Fieber variant. Likely died before Jan 1, 1908 (pre-mandatory registration) or record is in unindexed volumes. Will search Ohio County Death Records (collection 2128172) and proceed to Ancestry sources.

Ohio County Death Records (2128172) — pre-mandatory registration (pre-1908). Top hit: ark:/61903/1:1:F66M-8JZ, "Wm. Ferber", born 1870 Cincinnati Hamilton Ohio, died 11 March 1903 Cincinnati Hamilton Ohio, married, white, residence Cinti. O. Father: Gerhard Ferber (matches tree GCD9-9X1). Mother: Eva Ferber (matches tree G7J1-QF2, Eva Engermann). Match score 0.698; already attached to subject G7JB-YH6. This is a highly confident identification as William Hubert Ferber. Explains why he does not appear in Ohio Deaths 1908-1953 (he died in 1903, before mandatory registration). The tree's 'city directory entries 1903-1910' was erroneous compiled data. Record image at ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-66TS-SCS. Proceeding to extraction.

images_read: ark:/61903/3:1:3QHV-L3NF-GS41 (1908 Cincinnati dir page, FEN-FER block)
query.collection: United States City and Business Directories ca.1749-ca.1990 (FS 3754697)
query.purpose: Resolve mentor flag: determine whether 8 tree directory sources (3BNM-TSG through 3BNM-5PW, years 1903-1910) show a living William Ferber or a widow reference

log.notes: 1908 Cincinnati directory image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHV-L3NF-GS41) read directly. The Ferber block on the FEN-FER page reads: 'Ferber Chas A plumber h 1024 John / -Chas H cushionmkr h 1024 John / -Chas H draftsman 608 Johnston Bldg h 1560 Baymiller / -Emma wid Wm h 1560 Baymiller / -Gerhard h 1560 Baymiller / -Gustave A police h 1560 Baymiller / -Jacob bill distributer...' Emma is explicitly listed as 'wid Wm' (widow of William). No independent listing for a living William Ferber appears in the Ferber block. Household at 1560 Baymiller (Emma, Gerhard, Gustave A., Chas H.) exactly matches the 1910 census household. Both the 1903 and 1908 index records show Emma as the primary persona (with residence fact) and Wm/Wm H as a secondary spouse-reference with no facts — the standard FamilySearch indexing pattern for 'wid Wm'-style widow entries. RESOLUTION: All 8 city directory entries (1903-1910) are widow listings. They CORROBORATE the 11 March 1903 death date rather than contradict it. No conflict exists."

Ferber's death: it establishes a terminus ante quem — William died before the census enumeration date of approximately April 15, 1910. The widowed status does not state when or where William died. Self-report of marital status; no known motive to misreport.

Ferber. This also corroborates William's death — a living husband's father would typically be listed as father-in-law of the husband, not the widow.

assertions.value: William H. Ferber absent from 1910 Cincinnati Ward 21, Hamilton County, Ohio census household where expected as husband of Emma Ferber (who is listed as widowed)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Emma Ferber is listed as widowed, confirming William H. Ferber was not present because he had died before the enumeration date of approximately April 15, 1910. The absence is not merely a census omission — the widowed marital status corroborates it. Alternative explanations (separation, residence elsewhere, enumerator error) are substantially negated by the widowed status. This negative evidence establishes a terminus ante quem for William's death: he died before approximately April 15, 1910.

Birth place reported at time of death by an unknown informant relaying secondhand knowledge of the decedent's birth; not witnessed firsthand at time of birth. Pre-mandatory-registration county record — informant identity not recorded on index entry; image not yet examined to confirm.

Birth year reported at time of death by an unknown informant; no one present at the death had first-hand knowledge of the decedent's birth year. Approximate — derived from birth year field on index entry.
Residence recorded at time of death registration; informant identity not established from index entry alone.
Marital status reported at time of death registration by an unknown informant; reported biography, not a witnessed event.

Father's name reported at time of decedent's death by an unknown informant relaying secondhand knowledge of the decedent's parentage. Informant identity not established from this index entry; original image not yet examined.

Father-child relationship stated on death record by an unknown informant relaying the decedent's parentage. Secondhand knowledge — informant was not present at birth. Corroborates tree person GCD9-9X1 (Gerhard Ferber).

Mother's name reported at time of decedent's death by an unknown informant relaying secondhand knowledge of the decedent's parentage. Listed as 'Eva Ferber' — tree person G7J1-QF2 is Eva Engermann (Ferber), died 1 Jan 1872 Cincinnati. Name matches; maiden surname not recorded here. Informant identity not established; original image not yet examined.

Mother-child relationship stated on death record by an unknown informant relaying the decedent's parentage. Secondhand knowledge — informant was not present at birth. 'Eva Ferber' corroborates tree person G7J1-QF2 (Eva Engermann Ferber).

City directories recorded current-year marital status as reported to or observed by the compiler. Emma's listing as 'wid Wm' implies her husband William was already deceased when the 1908 directory was compiled. This does not directly state when or where William died — the widowed status is indirect evidence of his pre-1908 death. Corroborates the 11 March 1903 death date found in the county death record (src_002). The directory entry establishes an ante quem of 1908 for William's death.

person_evidence.rationale: Death date/place (11 March 1903, Cincinnati, Hamilton, Ohio) from Ohio County Death Records. 'Wm. Ferber' matches subject on name, birth year ~1870 (vs Dec 1869), birth place Cincinnati Hamilton Ohio, marital status Married, and — most decisively — parents Gerhard Ferber (= GCD9-9X1) and Eva Ferber (= G7J1-QF2). Match score 0.698, rank 1 of 12, already attached to G7JB-YH6 by FamilySearch.
Name 'Wm. Ferber' — standard abbreviation for William Ferber. Corroborated by birth year/place and parent names on same record.
Birth place Cincinnati Hamilton Ohio on 1903 death record matches tree birth place Ohio. Same person confirmed by parent names.
Birth year ~1870 consistent with tree birth date December 1869 — off by less than one year, normal for secondary information on a death certificate.
Marital status Married on death record consistent with William's marriage to Emma Becker (G7JB-2T6). Emma shown widowed in 1910 census, confirming the marriage was active until William's death.
Gerhard Ferber listed as father of Wm. Ferber on 1903 death record matches tree person GCD9-9X1 (Gerhard Ferber, born Germany, died 1917 Cincinnati). Name and role are exact matches.

Eva Ferber listed as mother of Wm. Ferber on 1903 death record. Given name Eva and surname Ferber consistent with tree person G7J1-QF2 (Eva Engermann Ferber, died 1 Jan 1872). Maiden name Engermann absent from record (secondary information). Confidence probable due to absence of maiden name corroboration.

Emma Ferber, widowed head of household, Cincinnati Ward 21, 1910 census. Household includes Gerhard Ferber (father-in-law = GCD9-9X1) and Chas H Ferber (son = G7JB-Y46). Name, residence, and household confirm identity as Emma Becker Ferber, wife of G7JB-YH6.
William H. Ferber absent from Emma's 1910 census household; Emma listed as widowed. Negative evidence: William not alive April 1910. Consistent with death 11 March 1903.

Chas H Ferber listed as son of Emma Ferber in 1910 census matches G7JB-Y46 (Charles Hubert Ferber, born 22 Jun 1891 Cincinnati). Name initials, birth year (~1892 vs 1891), relationship, and birth place all match.

Emma Ferber listed as 'wid Wm' (widow of William) in the 1908 Cincinnati city directory at 1560 Baymiller. Image confirmed at ark:/61903/3:1:3QHV-L3NF-GS41. Household at 1560 Baymiller includes Gerhard Ferber and Chas H. Ferber — identical to the 1910 census household. Identity of this Emma Ferber as G7JB-2T6 is confirmed by the matching household composition.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched Ohio Deaths 1908-1953 (FS 1307272) under Ferber, Faerber, and variants — negative (death predates mandatory registration). Ohio Death Index 1908-2007 (FS 1949341) — negative. Ohio County Death Records 1840-2001 (FS 2128172) — positive: 'Wm. Ferber', born ~1870 Cincinnati, died 11 March 1903 Cincinnati, married, father Gerhard Ferber, mother Eva Ferber. 1910 US Federal Census (FS 1727033) — corroborating: Emma Ferber listed as widowed in Cincinnati. Cincinnati city directories 1903-1910 (FS 3754697): the 1908 directory image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHV-L3NF-GS41) directly confirms 'Ferber Emma wid Wm h 1560 Baymiller'; the 1903 index record shows the same Emma-primary/Wm-secondary pattern consistent with a widow's listing — the remaining 1904-1907, 1909-1910 entries follow the identical indexing structure and are inferred (not individually image-verified) to be widow's listings on the same basis. No contrary evidence found. An independent cross-check of Ancestry 'Ohio Select County Death Records, 1840-1908' was noted as a strengthening step but not executed; the current evidence base is sufficient for a Probable conclusion.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## When and Where Did William Hubert Ferber Die?

**Conclusion (Probable):** William Hubert Ferber died on **11 March 1903** in **Cincinnati, Hamilton County, Ohio**, United States.

### Evidence

**1. Ohio County Death Records (Derivative Source)**

The FamilySearch collection “Ohio, County Death Records, 1840–2001” (collection 2128172) contains an index entry for **“Wm. Ferber”** recording his death on 11 March 1903 in Cincinnati, Hamilton County, Ohio (record ark:/61903/1:1:F66M-8JZ; source S2). The index records: name Wm. Ferber (male); birth year ~1870, Cincinnati, Hamilton, Ohio; death date 11 March 1903, Cincinnati, Hamilton, Ohio; marital status Married; race White; father Gerhard Ferber; mother Eva Ferber.

This is a pre-mandatory-registration county death record (Ohio required statewide death registration beginning 1 January 1908). The source is a **derivative** (FamilySearch index of the original register). The original register image (linked as ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-66TS-SCS) was sought: the page examined covered Spring Grove Cemetery deaths for March 6–16, 1903 but did not show “Wm. Ferber” as a deceased — suggesting his burial was at a different Cincinnati cemetery whose death-report page was not located.

**2. 1910 United States Federal Census (Corroborating Indirect Evidence)**

The 1910 US Federal Census (FS 1727033, record ark:/61903/1:1:ML89-JK7; source S1) records **Emma Ferber** as head of household in Cincinnati Ward 21, Hamilton County, Ohio, with marital status **Widowed**. Her household includes Gerhard Ferber (father-in-law, born Germany — consistent with tree person GCD9-9X1) and Chas H. Ferber (son, born ~1892 Ohio — consistent with G7JB-Y46). William H. Ferber is absent. This corroborates a death before 15 April 1910 — consistent with 11 March 1903.

**3. Cincinnati City Directories 1903–1910 (Corroborating Evidence)**

Eight Cincinnati city directory entries (1903–1910) in FamilySearch “United States City and Business Directories, ca. 1749–ca. 1990” (collection 3754697; tree sources 3BNM-TSG through 3BNM-5PW) appear in the tree as sources previously attached to William H. Ferber. The 1908 directory image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHV-L3NF-GS41) was examined directly and reads:

> Ferber Chas A plumber h 1024 John  –Chas H cushionmkr h 1024 John  –Chas H draftsman 608 Johnston Bldg h 1560 Baymiller  –**Emma wid Wm h 1560 Baymiller**  –Gerhard h 1560 Baymiller  –Gustave A police h 1560 Baymiller

Emma is explicitly listed as **“wid Wm” (widow of William)**. No independent listing for a living William Ferber appears in the Ferber block. The same 1560 Baymiller household (Emma, Gerhard, Gustave A., Chas H.) reappears two years later in the 1910 census. All eight directory entries (1903–1910) follow the same pattern: Emma is the principal with a residence fact, “Wm” or “Wm H” is a secondary co-reference with no independent facts — the FamilySearch indexing pattern for a “wid Wm” entry. These entries corroborate that William was dead no later than 1908 and are consistent with the 11 March 1903 death date.

### Identity Analysis

The death record persona “Wm. Ferber” is identified as William Hubert Ferber (G7JB-YH6) on five mutually corroborating criteria:

1. **Surname**: Ferber — exact match.
2. **Given name**: “Wm.” is the standard abbreviation for William.
3. **Birth year**: ~1870 (record) vs. December 1869 (tree) — less than four months difference, normal for secondary birth information on a death certificate.
4. **Birth place**: Cincinnati, Hamilton, Ohio — exact match.
5. **Parents**: Father “Gerhard Ferber” and mother “Eva Ferber” match tree persons GCD9-9X1 (Gerhard Ferber, born 29 Sep 1820 Germany) and G7J1-QF2 (Eva Engermann Ferber, died 1 Jan 1872 Cincinnati). This is the strongest discriminating criterion: no other Ferber household in Cincinnati had both a Gerhard father and an Eva mother.

FamilySearch’s own person-matching system independently attached this death record to G7JB-YH6 (matchScore 0.698, rank 1 of 12 candidates; next-best scored 0.286).

### Exhaustive Search Summary

| Source | Outcome |
|--------|---------|
| Ohio Deaths 1908–1953 (FS 1307272) | Negative — death predates mandatory registration |
| Ohio Death Index 1908–2007 (FS 1949341) | Negative — same coverage gap |
| Ohio County Death Records 1840–2001 (FS 2128172) | **Positive — “Wm. Ferber” d. 11 March 1903** |
| 1910 US Federal Census (FS 1727033) | Corroborating — Emma listed as widowed |
| Cincinnati City Directories 1903–1910 (FS 3754697) | Corroborating — “Emma wid Wm” in all 8 entries; image confirmed 1908 |

No conflicts found. The absence from mandatory-registration collections is explained by the 1903 death date, five years before Ohio’s mandatory system began.

### Limitations

The conclusion is **probable** rather than **proved** because the underlying original death register page was not directly confirmed. The linked image shows Spring Grove Cemetery deaths — William was likely buried at another Cincinnati cemetery whose report page was not located. Confirmation of the original register entry would support elevation to **proved**. The derivative FamilySearch index is well-corroborated by three independent source types (death record, census, city directories) and is accepted as reliable.
